options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Fatty acids can have more than two double bonds. Some polyunsaturated fatty acids can have up to six double bonds.
This option is incorrect. While some fatty acids may have three double bonds, the maximum number is higher.
This option is incorrect. Although some fatty acids may contain four double bonds, the maximum can be greater than this.
This option is correct. Some unsaturated fatty acids can have up to six double bonds, especially long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acids.

questions.explanation: Fatty acids are carboxylic acids that can be saturated or unsaturated. Unsaturated fatty acids contain one or more double bonds. While some fatty acids might have a few double bonds, certain long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acids, such as those found in fish oils, can have up to six double bonds. Options A, B, and C are incorrect because they underestimate the maximum number of double bonds possible in fatty acids.

questions.text: An example of a saturated fatty acid is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Palmitic acid is one of the most common saturated fatty acids in the body and in many foods. It has a 16-carbon backbone (written as C16:0), which means all the carbon atoms are fully saturated with hydrogen. Because it has no double bonds, the chain stays straight and packs tightly, which is why saturated fats are usually solid at room temperature.
Linoleic acid is an unsaturated fatty acid with 18 carbon atoms and two double bonds, located at C9 and C12.
Oleic acid is an unsaturated fatty acid with 18 carbon atoms and one double bond at C9.
Erucic acid is an unsaturated fatty acid with 22 carbon atoms and one double bond in the chain.

questions.explanation: Palmitic acid is a saturated fatty acid as it contains no double bonds between carbon atoms. Saturated fatty acids are characterized by having only single bonds between carbon atoms, which makes them 'saturated' with hydrogen atoms. In contrast, the other options are unsaturated fatty acids: Linoleic acid has two double bonds, Oleic acid has one double bond, and Erucic acid has one double bond as well, making them all unsaturated.

questions.text: Minimum amount of energy is stored by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lipids are a diverse class of organic molecules that are essential to life and play various important roles in living organisms. Unlike carbohydrates and proteins, lipids are not defined by a specific chemical structure, but rather by their hydrophobic (water-repelling) properties.Lipids are hydrophobic molecules, which means they do not readily dissolve in water but are soluble in nonpolar solvents, such as organic solvents like ether, chloroform, and oils.

Proteins are large, complex macromolecules made up of amino acids, and they are essential to the structure and function of all living cells. They are involved in a wide range of biological processes and perform numerous critical roles in the human body.Proteins are composed of amino acids, which are the building blocks of these macromolecules. There are 20 standard amino acids that can be combined in various sequences to create a wide array of proteins.

Nucleic acids are one of the two major classes of macromolecules (the other being proteins) and are essential to the storage and transmission of genetic information in living organisms. They come in two primary forms: deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) and ribonucleic acid (RNA).

Carbohydrates are one of the four major classes of organic compounds in living organisms, along with proteins, lipids (fats), and nucleic acids. Carbohydrates are composed of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen atoms and serve as a primary source of energy for both plants and animals. They play essential roles in various biological processes.

questions.explanation: Nucleic acids are one of the two major classes of macromolecules (the other being proteins) and are essential to the storage and transmission of genetic information in living organisms. They come in two primary forms: deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) and ribonucleic acid (RNA).

questions.text: Which of these labels matches the structure of the partial RNA strand shown?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: I. Phosphate; II. Ribose; III. Nitrogenous base
options.explanation: This option is correct because it accurately describes the components of an RNA nucleotide in the correct order: Phosphate group, Ribose sugar, and Nitrogenous base.

options.text: I. Phosphate; II. Nitrogen base; III. Ribose
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it incorrectly positions the nitrogenous base as the second component instead of the ribose sugar.

options.text: I. Nitrogenous base; II. Phosphate; III. Ribose
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it incorrectly positions the nitrogenous base as the first component and the phosphate group as the second.

options.text: I. Ribose; II. Nitrogen base; III. Phosphate
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it incorrectly positions the ribose as the first component and the phosphate group as the last.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: ﻿I. Phosphate; II. Ribose; III. Nitrogenous base. This sequence accurately represents the structure of an RNA nucleotide, where the phosphate group attaches to the ribose sugar, which is connected to the nitrogenous base.The other options are incorrect because they misplace one or more of these components in the sequence of the RNA structure:Option B incorrectly places the nitrogenous base before the ribose.Option C incorrectly lists the nitrogenous base first and the phosphate second.Option D incorrectly lists the ribose first and the phosphate last.

questions.text: It is strongly associated with ribosomal protein where 40 to 50% of it is present:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Messenger RNA (mRNA) carries genetic information from the DNA to the ribosome, but it is not a structural component of ribosomes. It is not associated with ribosomal proteins at the level of 40 to 50%.

Ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is a key structural component of ribosomes and is associated with ribosomal proteins, making up about 40 to 50% of ribosome's mass. It is integral to the function and structure of ribosomes.
Small nuclear RNA (snRNA) is involved in the splicing of pre-mRNA in the nucleus and does not have a direct structural association with ribosomal proteins.

Transfer RNA (tRNA) is involved in bringing amino acids to the ribosome during protein synthesis but is not a structural component of the ribosome itself and is not associated with ribosomal proteins in significant quantities.

questions.explanation: Ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is the correct answer because it is a fundamental structural component of ribosomes, where it forms a significant part of the ribosome's mass, approximately 40 to 50%, alongside ribosomal proteins. Unlike mRNA, tRNA, and snRNA, rRNA's primary role is not in carrying genetic information or splicing but in forming the ribosome and facilitating protein synthesis.mRNA is incorrect as it serves to convey genetic information rather than forming part of the ribosome. snRNA is involved in RNA processing within the nucleus, not in the ribosome's structure. tRNA functions in translation by delivering amino acids to the ribosome and is not a structural component like rRNA.

questions.text: The RNA molecules mostly occur as a:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The term 'duplex' typically refers to a double-stranded structure. This is more characteristic of DNA, which forms a stable double helix. RNA, however, usually does not form such extensive duplex structures in its functional state.

While some RNA molecules can form double-stranded regions through complementary base pairing, they predominantly exist as single-stranded molecules. Double-stranded RNA does occur in certain cases, such as in some viruses, but it is not the most common form.
RNA molecules are primarily single-stranded, allowing them to fold into complex three-dimensional shapes necessary for their diverse biological functions, such as catalysis and regulation.

While RNA can serve as genetic material in certain viruses, the term 'genetic material' is not a structural description. It refers to the role nucleic acids play in heredity, which applies to both single-stranded and double-stranded forms.

questions.explanation: RNA is primarily known to exist as a single-stranded structure. This configuration enables RNA to perform various functions, including catalysis, gene regulation, and serving as a template for protein synthesis. While RNA can form double-stranded regions through intramolecular base pairing, these are localized structures within an overall single-stranded molecule. In contrast, duplex and double-stranded structures are characteristic of DNA, which forms stable double helices. Although RNA can act as genetic material in some viruses, this option does not address the structural format of RNA molecules.

questions.text: All three types of_are synthesized from_ in the_:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option correctly identifies that all three types of RNA (mRNA, tRNA, and rRNA) are synthesized from DNA within the nucleus of eukaryotic cells. DNA serves as a template for RNA synthesis during transcription, which occurs in the nucleus.
This option incorrectly suggests that RNA is synthesized from the nucleus within DNA. The correct sequence is that RNA is synthesized from DNA within the nucleus, not the other way around.
This option incorrectly implies that DNA is synthesized from RNA within the nucleus. RNA is synthesized from DNA, not the other way around, making this option incorrect.

This option incorrectly suggests that the nucleus is synthesized from RNA within DNA. The nucleus is an organelle where DNA resides, and RNA is synthesized from DNA within it, thus this order is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that all three types of RNA (mRNA, tRNA, and rRNA) are synthesized from DNA in the nucleus. The nucleus houses the DNA, which serves as a template for RNA synthesis during transcription. The other options are incorrect because they reverse or misrepresent the biological sequence of synthesis—RNA is synthesized from DNA within the nucleus, not the other way around. This process is crucial for protein synthesis, as RNA molecules are key players in translating genetic information into functional proteins.

questions.text: It comprises about 10 to 20% of the cellular RNA:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Messenger RNA (mRNA) accounts for a small percentage of the total cellular RNA. It serves as a temporary copy of genetic instructions carried from the DNA in the nucleus to the ribosomes for protein synthesis.

Transfer RNA (tRNA) makes up about 10 to 20% of the total cellular RNA. It is responsible for bringing the correct amino acids to the ribosome, matching them to the mRNA code during protein synthesis.
Ribosomal RNA (rRNA) constitutes the majority of cellular RNA, approximately 80%, as it forms the core structural and functional components of ribosomes.
Small nuclear RNA (snRNA) is involved in the splicing of pre-mRNA but comprises only a minor fraction of the total cellular RNA.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is tRNA. Transfer RNA (tRNA) constitutes about 10 to 20% of the total cellular RNA. It plays a crucial role in protein synthesis by transporting specific amino acids to the ribosome, where they are added to the growing polypeptide chain according to the sequence dictated by the mRNA.Option mRNA is incorrect because it makes up a smaller percentage of the total RNA, serving as a temporary messenger carrying instructions from DNA to the ribosome.Option rRNA is incorrect because it accounts for approximately 80% of cellular RNA and forms the structural components of ribosomes.The option snRNA is incorrect because it is involved in mRNA processing and constitutes only a small proportion of the total RNA.

questions.text: It is the major form of RNA in the cell:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is the most abundant form of RNA in the cell. It constitutes the structural and functional components of ribosomes, which are essential for protein synthesis. This makes rRNA the major form of RNA in terms of both quantity and function.

Transfer RNA (tRNA) is crucial for translating genetic information into proteins, but it is not the most abundant form of RNA in the cell. Its primary role is to carry amino acids to the ribosome during protein synthesis.

Messenger RNA (mRNA) serves as the template for protein synthesis, conveying genetic information from DNA to the ribosome. While it plays a critical role, it is not the most abundant form of RNA in the cell.
Small nuclear RNA (snRNA) is involved in the splicing of pre-mRNA, a different aspect of gene expression. It is not the major form of RNA in the cell.

questions.explanation: Ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is the most abundant form of RNA in the cell. It forms the core structural and functional components of ribosomes, which are essential for protein synthesis. This abundance is due to the large quantity of ribosomes required for protein production. In contrast, transfer RNA (tRNA) and messenger RNA (mRNA) play specific roles in protein synthesis but are not as abundant as rRNA. Small nuclear RNA (snRNA) is involved in RNA processing, specifically splicing, and is not directly involved in protein synthesis, nor is it the major form of RNA in the cell.

questions.text: On the surface of _, the mRNA and tRNA interact to translate the information from genes into a specific protein.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: DNA contains the genetic blueprint for the organism, but it does not directly participate in the translation process. DNA is located in the nucleus (in eukaryotes) and serves as the template for mRNA synthesis during transcription, not translation.

Chromosomes are structures made of DNA and protein that contain many genes. While crucial for storing genetic information, chromosomes are not involved in the translation process, as this occurs outside the nucleus on ribosomes.

Ribosomes are the cellular structures where protein synthesis occurs. They facilitate the interaction between mRNA and tRNA to translate genetic information into proteins, making them the correct choice for this question.

Nucleosomes are units of DNA packaging in eukaryotic cells, consisting of DNA wrapped around histone proteins. They are involved in the organization and compaction of DNA but are not directly involved in the translation process.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the ribosome. Ribosomes are the sites within the cell where translation occurs, which is the process of synthesizing proteins from mRNA. Ribosomes facilitate the interaction between mRNA and tRNA, enabling the translation of genetic information into a specific protein sequence. DNA and chromosomes are involved in storing and transmitting genetic information but do not directly participate in the translation process. Nucleosomes are related to DNA packaging and organization, not translation.

questions.text: In an amino acid, in which the R group is hydrogen, the amino acid will be:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Alanine has a side chain consisting of a methyl group (-CH₃), not just a hydrogen. It is a non-essential amino acid that the body can synthesize, but it is not the simplest amino acid.
Glycine is the simplest amino acid, with its R group being a single hydrogen atom. It is a non-essential amino acid, meaning the body can produce it.
Leucine has a branched aliphatic side chain, making it a branched-chain amino acid (BCAA). It is essential and must be obtained through the diet, but its R group is not hydrogen.
Valine is another branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) with an aliphatic side chain. It is essential, meaning the body cannot synthesize it, and its R group is not hydrogen.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Glycine. Glycine is the simplest amino acid because its R group is a single hydrogen atom. This makes it unique among the standard amino acids. Alanine, Leucine, and Valine all have more complex side chains with different chemical structures and properties. Alanine's side chain is a methyl group, while Leucine and Valine have branched aliphatic side chains, classifying them as branched-chain amino acids (BCAAs). Glycine, being the simplest, is essential for forming protein structures that require minimal steric hindrance.

questions.text: Select the molecular formula of lactose.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. It does not correspond to the molecular formula of lactose. C₁₁H₂₂O₁₁ is the molecular formula for many disaccharides because it represents two monosaccharides joined together with the loss of one molecule of water.Common examples with this formula include:• Sucrose • Lactose • MaltoseThey all share the same formula but differ in the types of monosaccharides involved and how they are linked. This difference in bonding changes their structure, sweetness, and how they are digested.
This option is correct. This is the molecular formula of lactose, which is a disaccharide composed of glucose and galactose.

This option is incorrect. It suggests an incorrect ratio of hydrogen and oxygen atoms, not aligning with lactose's structure. C₁₂H₂₄O₁₂ is not a typical formula for common biological sugars.If you look at the pattern for carbohydrates, the general empirical formula is (CH₂O)ₙ. For n = 12, that would be C₁₂H₂₄O₁₂, so it fits the basic carbohydrate pattern but does not match common mono- or disaccharides.
This option is incorrect. The carbon count is inconsistent with lactose's structure. C₁₁H₂₄O₁₂ does not correspond to any common biological carbohydrate, lipid, or metabolite.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct because it accurately represents the molecular formula of lactose, C12H22O11, which is a disaccharide composed of glucose and galactose units. Option A is incorrect because it lacks one carbon atom, not matching the structure of lactose. Option C is incorrect due to an inaccurate hydrogen and oxygen ratio, and Option D is incorrect because both the carbon count and the hydrogen-oxygen ratio do not align with lactose's known structure.

questions.text: Except one, all carbon atoms in a monosaccharide have the following group.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A hydroxyl group is a functional group (-OH) consisting of an oxygen atom covalently bonded to a hydrogen atom. It is commonly found in organic compounds and is the defining feature of alcohols. The hydroxyl group imparts specific chemical properties to the compounds that contain it.

An amino group is a functional group in organic chemistry that contains a nitrogen atom bonded to two hydrogen atoms. The amino group is a crucial component of amino acids, the building blocks of proteins, and is commonly found in a variety of organic compounds.

An aldehydic group, or aldehyde group, is a functional group in organic chemistry characterized by a carbonyl group (a carbon-oxygen double bond, C=O) with a hydrogen atom (H) directly attached to the carbonyl carbon. The general formula for an aldehyde group is RCHO, where R represents an organic group.

An aldehydic group, or aldehyde group, is a functional group in organic chemistry characterized by a carbonyl group (a carbon-oxygen double bond, C=O) with a hydrogen atom (H) directly attached to the carbonyl carbon. The general formula for an aldehyde group is RCHO, where R represents an organic group. A ketonic group, or ketone group, is a functional group in organic chemistry characterized by a carbonyl group (a carbon-oxygen double bond, C=O) with two organic groups attached to the carbonyl carbon. The general formula for a ketone group is RCOR', where R and R' represent different organic groups.

questions.explanation: A hydroxyl group is a functional group (-OH) consisting of an oxygen atom covalently bonded to a hydrogen atom. It is commonly found in organic compounds and is the defining feature of alcohols. The hydroxyl group imparts specific chemical properties to the compounds that contain it.

questions.text: Select the following which is hexoses:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribose is a five-carbon sugar, known as a pentose. Its structure includes five carbon atoms, making it ineligible as a hexose.
Erythrose is a four-carbon sugar, classified as a tetrose. It does not have six carbon atoms, so it is not a hexose.
Glucoheptose is a seven-carbon sugar, known as a heptose. With more than six carbons, it is not considered a hexose.
Galactose is a six-carbon sugar, making it a hexose. It is commonly found in milk and dairy products as part of the disaccharide lactose.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Galactose. Galactose is a monosaccharide that contains six carbon atoms, classifying it as a hexose. It is often found in combination with glucose to form lactose, a disaccharide in milk. In contrast, Ribose is a five-carbon sugar (pentose), Erythrose is a four-carbon sugar (tetrose), and Glucoheptose is a seven-carbon sugar (heptose). Therefore, none of these are hexoses.

questions.text: Which of the following statement is incorrect for fats containing unsaturated fatty acid?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fats, both saturated and unsaturated, have a lower density than water. Their molecular structure makes them less compact, so they float rather than sink. This is why oils form a layer on top of water.
This option is correct for unsaturated fatty acids, as they inherently have one or more double bonds in their hydrocarbon chain.

options.text: They are usually solid at room temperature
options.explanation: This option is incorrect for unsaturated fats, which are usually liquid at room temperature. Their double bonds create bends in the fatty acid chains, stopping them from packing closely together. This loose packing lowers the melting point and keeps them in a liquid form.

options.text: Their specific gravity is less than one
options.explanation: Like most fats, unsaturated fats have a specific gravity below one, so they are less dense than water. This is why they float and form a separate layer when mixed with water.

questions.explanation: Fats containing unsaturated fatty acids are generally liquid at room temperature because the double bonds introduce kinks in the fatty acid chains, preventing them from packing closely together. This property contrasts with saturated fats, which are solid at room temperature due to their ability to pack tightly without double bonds. Therefore, the statement that they are usually solid at room temperature is incorrect. Options stating that they contain double bonds and have specific gravity less than one are true characteristics of unsaturated fats, while the statement about being lighter than water is generally true for all fats.

questions.text: Phosphatidic acid is composed of :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. Phosphatidic acid forms the backbone for many phospholipids, consisting of one glycerol molecule, two fatty acid chains, and one phosphoric acid group.

options.text: Glycerol + Fatty acid + Phosphoric acid + N-base
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Phosphatidic acid does not include any nitrogenous base; it is strictly composed of glycerol, two fatty acids, and phosphoric acid.
This option is incorrect. Phosphatidic acid contains only one glycerol molecule, not two. It is composed of a glycerol backbone, two fatty acid chains, and one phosphoric acid group.

options.text: Glycerol + Fatty acid + 2 Phosphoric acid + N-base
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Phosphatidic acid contains only one phosphoric acid group and no nitrogenous base. Its structure includes one glycerol, two fatty acids, and one phosphoric acid.

questions.explanation: Phosphatidic acid is a crucial intermediate in lipid biosynthesis and serves as a fundamental building block for phospholipids. It is composed of a glycerol backbone bonded to two fatty acid chains and one phosphoric acid group. This specific structure distinguishes it from other lipid molecules that may contain additional components, such as nitrogenous bases or extra glycerol or phosphoric acid groups. Options B, C, and D propose structures with constituents not found in phosphatidic acid, leading to their incorrectness. Option B incorrectly includes a nitrogenous base, Option C incorrectly proposes two glycerol molecules, and Option D inaccurately suggests two phosphoric acid groups and a nitrogenous base.

questions.text: Which of the following is cloverleaf like structure?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Messenger RNA (mRNA) is a linear molecule that carries genetic information from DNA to ribosomes for protein synthesis. It does not have a cloverleaf structure.
DNA is made of two strands that twist around each other to form a helix. It does not have a cloverleaf shape. Its role is to store and pass on genetic information in every cell.
Transfer RNA (tRNA) has a distinctive cloverleaf structure. It functions as an adaptor molecule, matching amino acids to the corresponding mRNA codons during protein synthesis.
Ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is a fundamental component of ribosomes and does not have a cloverleaf structure. It assists in protein synthesis by ensuring the proper alignment of mRNA and tRNA.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is tRNA. Transfer RNA (tRNA) has a characteristic cloverleaf structure formed by its secondary structure, which includes three loops and an anticodon that pairs with the mRNA codon. This structure is essential for its function in translation, as it brings the correct amino acid to the ribosome during protein synthesis. The other options - mRNA, DNA, and rRNA - have different structures and functions. mRNA is linear and serves as a template for protein synthesis, DNA is a double helix that contains the genetic code, and rRNA is a component of ribosomes without a cloverleaf structure.

questions.text: Glucose is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: An aldose is a monosaccharide with an aldehyde group, and a hexose has six carbon atoms. Glucose, being a six-carbon sugar with an aldehyde group, is classified as an aldose hexose sugar.
A furanose pentose sugar is a five-membered ring structure and has five carbon atoms. Glucose does not fit this classification as it has six carbon atoms and forms a pyranose ring.
A ketose has a ketone group. Glucose is not a ketose because it contains an aldehyde group, not a ketone group. Fructose is an example of a ketose hexose sugar.
A pyranose refers to a six-membered ring structure, but a pentose has five carbon atoms. Glucose forms a pyranose ring but has six carbon atoms, so it is not a pentose sugar.

questions.explanation: Glucose is classified as an aldose hexose sugar because it contains an aldehyde group (making it an aldose) and has six carbon atoms (making it a hexose). The other options are incorrect because:Furanose pentose sugar: Glucose is not a furanose pentose as it does not form a five-membered ring nor does it have five carbon atoms.Ketose hexose sugar: Glucose contains an aldehyde group, not a ketone, which is characteristic of ketoses like fructose.Pyranose Pentose sugar: While glucose can form a pyranose ring, it is not a pentose as it has six carbon atoms.

questions.text: Oligosaccharides contain:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incorrect. While disaccharides, a subset of oligosaccharides, contain two monosaccharides, oligosaccharides can contain up to 9 monosaccharides upon hydrolysis.
That statement is not correct. By definition, oligosaccharides release between two and nine monosaccharide units when they are hydrolyzed.
Correct. Oligosaccharides yield 2 to 9 monosaccharide units upon hydrolysis. They can be categorized into disaccharides, trisaccharides, etc., based on the number of monosaccharides they contain.
Incorrect. This description applies to polysaccharides, not oligosaccharides. Polysaccharides yield a large number of monosaccharides upon hydrolysis.

questions.explanation: Oligosaccharides are carbohydrates that, upon hydrolysis, yield between 2 to 9 monosaccharide units. This distinguishes them from monosaccharides, which do not hydrolyze further, and polysaccharides, which yield many monosaccharides. Therefore, the correct answer is that oligosaccharides contain 2-9 monosaccharides. The other options are incorrect because they either describe a subset (disaccharides) or a different type of carbohydrate (polysaccharides).

questions.text: Reducing sugars are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Glucose, fructose, galactose, maltose, and lactose
options.explanation: This option is correct because all of these sugars possess a free aldehydic or ketonic group, which enables them to act as reducing agents. Glucose, fructose, and galactose are monosaccharides, while maltose and lactose are disaccharides that retain a free anomeric carbon capable of reducing other compounds.

This option is incorrect. While glucose is a reducing sugar, sucrose is not because its glycosidic bond involves both anomeric carbons, preventing it from acting as a reducing agent. Cellulose is a polysaccharide and does not have free anomeric carbons available for reduction.

This option is incorrect. Although lactose is a reducing sugar, starch and trehalose are not. Trehalose is a non-reducing disaccharide because its glycosidic bond involves both anomeric carbons, and starch is a polysaccharide with no free anomeric carbon available for reduction.
This option is incorrect. Sucrose is a non-reducing sugar because its glycosidic bond involves both anomeric carbons, leaving no free group to act as a reducing agent.

questions.explanation: Reducing sugars are those sugars that can donate electrons to another chemical, effectively reducing it, while they themselves become oxidized. This property is due to the presence of a free aldehydic or ketonic group. All monosaccharides are reducing sugars, as are some disaccharides, such as maltose and lactose, which have a free anomeric carbon. Sucrose does not exhibit this behavior because both anomeric carbons are involved in the glycosidic bond, rendering it non-reducing. Similarly, polysaccharides like starch and cellulose lack free anomeric carbons needed for reduction.

questions.text: If deoxyribose sugar is supplemented with oxygen at the second carbon atom, which one of these is formed?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect because erythrose is a tetrose sugar with 4 carbon atoms. Deoxyribose, when supplemented with oxygen at the second carbon, becomes ribose, a pentose sugar with 5 carbon atoms.
This is incorrect because ribulose is a ketopentose, whereas deoxyribose is an aldopentose. Adding an oxygen atom to the second carbon of deoxyribose results in ribose, not ribulose.
This is incorrect because heptose is a sugar with 7 carbon atoms. Deoxyribose has 5 carbon atoms, and adding oxygen to the second carbon does not change the number of carbon atoms. It forms ribose.
This is correct because deoxyribose is essentially ribose lacking an oxygen atom at the second carbon. When oxygen is added to this position, ribose is formed.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Ribose. Deoxyribose is similar to ribose but lacks an oxygen atom at the second carbon. When this deficiency is corrected by adding an oxygen atom to the second carbon, deoxyribose becomes ribose. The other options are incorrect: Erythrose is a tetrose with only 4 carbon atoms, Ribulose is a ketopentose rather than an aldopentose, and Heptose has 7 carbon atoms, which doesn't match the structural framework of deoxyribose or ribose.

questions.text: The sweetest sugar is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fructose is the sweetest naturally occurring carbohydrate. It is significantly sweeter than both glucose and sucrose. Due to its high sweetness level, it is often used in lower quantities in food products, making it the correct choice.
Sucrose, commonly known as table sugar, is not as sweet as fructose. Fructose is approximately 1.2-1.8 times sweeter than sucrose, which makes this option incorrect.
Glucose is less sweet than fructose. In fact, fructose is about twice as sweet as glucose, making glucose an incorrect choice for the sweetest sugar.
Monellin is a protein, not a carbohydrate or sugar. Although it is extremely sweet, being 2000-3000 times sweeter than sucrose, it does not qualify as a sugar, making this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: Fructose is the sweetest naturally occurring carbohydrate and is significantly sweeter than both glucose and sucrose. This is why it is often used to sweeten foods. Sucrose, although sweet, does not match the sweetness level of fructose. Glucose is even less sweet compared to fructose. Monellin, despite its high sweetness, is a protein and not a sugar, thereby disqualifying it from being the correct answer.

questions.text: Deoxyribose is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the formula of ribose, a 5-carbon sugar with five oxygen atoms. Deoxyribose is similar but has one less oxygen atom.
This is the formula of glucose, a 6-carbon sugar or hexose, which is not related to deoxyribose.
This is the formula of deoxyribose. It is a 5-carbon sugar that is similar to ribose but with one less oxygen atom, which is why it is called 'deoxy'.
This is incorrect as it represents a hexose with an unusual oxygen count for hexoses. It does not correspond to deoxyribose.

questions.explanation: Deoxyribose is represented by the formula C5H10O4, indicating it is a 5-carbon sugar with one less oxygen atom than ribose, which has the formula C5H10O5. This loss of an oxygen atom is what gives it the name 'deoxy'. Option A is ribose, option B is glucose, and option D is not a standard sugar formula.

questions.text: Maltose is hydrolysed in the presence of maltase to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: When maltose is hydrolyzed by the enzyme maltase, it breaks down into two glucose molecules. Maltose is a disaccharide composed of two glucose units.
Fructose is not a component of maltose. Fructose is a monosaccharide found in other sugars like sucrose, which is composed of glucose and fructose.
This combination is incorrect for maltose. Maltose consists solely of two glucose units. Glucose and fructose are the components of sucrose, not maltose.
This is incorrect for maltose. Glucose and galactose are the components of lactose, not maltose. Maltose is composed of two glucose molecules.

questions.explanation: Maltose is a disaccharide composed of two glucose molecules. When hydrolyzed by the enzyme maltase, it breaks down into two glucose monosaccharides. Option A is correct because maltose is made up of glucose units. Option B is incorrect because fructose is not part of maltose; it is a component of sucrose. Option C is incorrect as maltose does not contain fructose. Option D is incorrect because glucose and galactose form lactose, not maltose.

questions.text: Iodine test is used to detect:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fats, or lipids, are not detected by the iodine test. The iodine test is specific to carbohydrates, particularly starches, which change color upon reaction with iodine.
Typhoid is a disease caused by a bacterial infection and is not related to carbohydrates. The iodine test does not detect diseases but is used to identify specific carbohydrates.
Malaria is a disease caused by parasites and has no connection to the iodine test or carbohydrates. The iodine test is used in the context of biochemistry to detect carbohydrates.

The iodine test is specifically used to detect carbohydrates, particularly starches, which turn blue-black when iodine is applied. This test does not detect other types of biological molecules like proteins or fats.

questions.explanation: The iodine test is a chemical test for the presence of starch, a type of carbohydrate. When iodine is added to a sample containing starch, it turns a blue-black color, indicating a positive result. This is because iodine interacts with the helical structure of amylose, a component of starch. The other options (fats, typhoid, and malaria) are unrelated to the iodine test: fats are a different class of biomolecules, and typhoid and malaria are diseases not detected by this test.

questions.text: How many atoms are there in pyranose ring?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A pyranose ring is a six-membered ring structure commonly found in sugars like glucose. Five of the atoms in the ring are carbons, and the sixth is an oxygen atom, which forms part of the ring itself.

A pyranose ring is a six-membered ring with five carbon atoms and one oxygen atom. It forms when a sugar’s carbonyl group reacts with a hydroxyl group, creating a cyclic structure. This contrasts with furanose rings, which have five members. Pyranose forms are common in sugars like glucose.
A pyranose ring is a six-membered ring made up of five carbons and one oxygen. It forms when a sugar’s carbonyl group reacts with a hydroxyl group, creating a cyclic structure.

A pyranose ring is a six-membered ring structure commonly found in sugars like glucose. Five of the atoms in the ring are carbons, and the sixth is an oxygen atom, which forms part of the ring itself. This ring structure results from the reaction of a sugar’s carbonyl group (aldehyde or ketone) with one of its hydroxyl groups, creating a cyclic hemiacetal (in aldoses) or hemiketal (in ketoses).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 6. A pyranose ring is a six-membered structure in organic chemistry, consisting of five carbon atoms and one oxygen atom. This configuration forms a stable ring, which is a common feature in carbohydrates such as glucose. Options A, C, and D are incorrect because they do not accurately represent the number of atoms in a pyranose ring.

questions.text: Which of the following are all disaccharides?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Maltose is a disaccharide composed of two glucose molecules. Sucrose is a disaccharide composed of one glucose molecule and one fructose molecule. Lactose is a disaccharide composed of one glucose molecule and one galactose molecule.
Glycogen is a polysaccharide, not a disaccharide. It is a storage form of glucose in animals. Lactose and sucrose are disaccharides, but including glycogen makes this option incorrect.
Glucose is a monosaccharide, not a disaccharide. Maltose and lactose are disaccharides, but including glucose makes this option incorrect.
This option is incorrect because not all the choices are correct. Only Option A contains only disaccharides.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: Maltose, Sucrose, Lactose. Each of these is a disaccharide, composed of two linked monosaccharides. Maltose consists of two glucose molecules, sucrose is made of glucose and fructose, and lactose is composed of glucose and galactose. Option B is incorrect because glycogen is a polysaccharide, not a disaccharide. Option C is incorrect because glucose is a monosaccharide, not a disaccharide. Option D is incorrect because not all options are correct; only Option A includes only disaccharides.

questions.text: α and β-glucose differ in the orientation of -OH group around:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: That’s incorrect. The α and β forms of glucose differ in the orientation of the hydroxyl group at carbon 1 (the anomeric carbon), not at carbon 3.

The α and β forms of glucose are called anomers, and they differ specifically at the anomeric carbon (C1). This is the carbon that was part of the carbonyl group (aldehyde in glucose) before the sugar cyclized to form a ring.
This option is correct. The distinction between α and β-glucose is based on the orientation of the -OH group at C1.
This option is incorrect. The difference in orientation between α and β-glucose is around C1, not C2.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C1 because α and β-glucose differ in the orientation of the hydroxyl (-OH) group at the anomeric carbon, which is C1. In α-glucose, the -OH group on C1 is positioned on the opposite side of the ring compared to the CH2OH group, while in β-glucose, it is on the same side. The other carbon positions (C2, C3, and C5) do not affect the anomeric nature of glucose, thus these options are incorrect.

questions.text: A sugar of animal origin is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fructose is a simple sugar found mainly in fruits, honey, and some vegetables. It is a plant-derived monosaccharide and is not naturally produced by animals.
Dihydroxyacetone phosphate (DHAP) is an intermediate in metabolic pathways like glycolysis and gluconeogenesis. It is not a sugar that occurs naturally in foods or animal products.
Lactose is a disaccharide made of glucose and galactose. It occurs naturally in milk and other dairy products, so it is considered an animal-derived sugar.
Phosphoglyceric acid (PGA) is an intermediate in the Calvin cycle during photosynthesis. It is not a sugar found in animal products and is primarily associated with plant metabolism.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Lactose. Lactose is a disaccharide sugar consisting of glucose and galactose. It is predominantly found in milk and dairy products, thus making it a sugar of animal origin.Fructose is a monosaccharide found in plants, particularly fruits, and not of animal origin. DHAP is not a sugar but a metabolic intermediate in glycolysis and gluconeogenesis, and it is not specific to animals. PGA refers to phosphoglyceric acid, which is involved in photosynthesis in plants, not in animals.

questions.text: Monosaccharide found in nucleolus is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pentose refers to a class of monosaccharides that contain five carbon atoms. Ribose and deoxyribose are common pentoses integral to nucleic acids, with ribose being a component of RNA found in the nucleolus.
Erythrose is a tetrose sugar, meaning it consists of four carbon atoms, and is not a component of nucleic acids or typically found in the nucleolus.
Tetrose describes monosaccharides with four carbon atoms. They do not include ribose or deoxyribose, which are essential for nucleic acids found in the nucleolus.
Hexose sugars contain six carbon atoms. They are not part of the nucleic acids that include ribose or deoxyribose, which are found in the nucleolus.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is pentose because ribose, a pentose sugar, is a component of RNA, which is synthesized in the nucleolus. The nucleolus is primarily involved in the production of ribosomes, which requires ribosomal RNA (rRNA). Hence, pentose sugars are integral to the structure of nucleic acids associated with the nucleolus. Erythrose and tetrose refer to sugars with four carbon atoms, which are not involved in nucleic acids. Hexose sugars have six carbon atoms and are primarily involved in energy processes rather than forming nucleic acids found in the nucleolus.

questions.text: A fat molecule has three fatty acids. A phospholipid molecule has how many fatty acids?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Unlike a triglyceride, which has three fatty acids, a phospholipid has two fatty acids and a phosphate group.
That’s incorrect. A phospholipid has two fatty acid chains attached to a glycerol backbone, along with a phosphate group, not just one fatty acid.
This option is correct. A phospholipid molecule typically consists of two fatty acids bonded to a glycerol backbone, along with a phosphate group.
That’s incorrect. A phospholipid does contain fatty acids—typically two fatty acid chains—attached to a glycerol backbone, along with a phosphate-containing group.

questions.explanation: A phospholipid molecule is a type of lipid that forms the basic structure of cell membranes. It contains two fatty acids attached to a glycerol backbone, whereas a triglyceride (fat molecule) has three fatty acids attached. The glycerol in phospholipids is also bonded to a phosphate group, which is not the case in triglycerides. Therefore, the correct answer is that a phospholipid has 2 fatty acids. Options indicating 0, 1, or 3 fatty acids are incorrect because they do not align with the typical structure of a phospholipid.

questions.text: The most abundant lipid in cell membrane is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phospholipids are the most abundant lipids in cell membranes. They form the lipid bilayer that provides the basic structure and barrier of the membrane. Each phospholipid molecule contains a hydrophilic head and two hydrophobic tails, which are crucial for the formation of lipid bilayers.

Cholesterol is an important component of cell membranes, contributing to membrane fluidity and stability, but it is not the most abundant lipid. It is interspersed among phospholipids in the bilayer but does not serve as the primary structural component.

Steroids, including cholesterol, are not the main structural lipids in cell membranes. They perform various roles, including acting as signaling molecules, but they do not form the primary structure of membranes.

Waxes are not components of cell membranes. They serve protective roles in nature, such as waterproofing and protection in plants and animals, but do not contribute to the structure of cell membranes.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is phospholipids. They are the most abundant lipids in cell membranes and are essential for forming the lipid bilayer, which is the fundamental structure of cell membranes. Phospholipids have unique properties, with hydrophilic heads and hydrophobic tails, allowing them to form bilayers in aqueous environments. Cholesterol, while crucial for membrane fluidity and integrity, is present in smaller amounts compared to phospholipids. Steroids and waxes do not form the structural basis of cell membranes, with steroids serving more as signaling molecules and waxes not being involved in cell membrane structure.

questions.text: Which one of the following gives maximum energy in metabolic processes?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Proteins supply essential amino acids needed for growth, repair, and other body functions. However, they provide less energy than fats, yielding about 4 kcal per gram compared to 9 kcal per gram from fats.
Fats are highly energy-dense, providing about 9 kcal per gram, making them the most efficient macronutrient for energy storage and production.
Nucleic acids are not used as a primary energy source; they are involved in genetic information storage and transfer.
Carbohydrates are a quick source of energy, providing 4 kcal per gram, but they are not as energy-dense as fats.

questions.explanation: Fats provide the most energy in metabolic processes because they yield approximately 9 kcal per gram, which is more than proteins and carbohydrates that provide about 4 kcal per gram. Nucleic acids are not considered a significant energy source. While carbohydrates are important for quick energy supply, fats are the most energy-dense, making them the preferred choice for long-term energy storage.

questions.text: CH3(CH2)7CH=CH(CH2)7COOH is formula of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Oxalosuccinate is an intermediate compound in the tricarboxylic acid (TCA) cycle, also known as the citric acid cycle or Krebs cycle. The TCA cycle is a central metabolic pathway that occurs in the mitochondria of eukaryotic cells and is responsible for the oxidation of acetyl-CoA to produce energy in the form of NADH and FADH2.

Linolenic acid is an essential polyunsaturated fatty acid belonging to the omega-3 fatty acid family. It is classified as an 18-carbon fatty acid with three double bonds, and its chemical structure is often represented as 18:3(n-3), indicating the number and position of the double bonds.Linolenic acid is an omega-3 fatty acid, which means that the first double bond is located at the third carbon from the methyl end of the carbon chain.

Oleic acid is a monounsaturated fatty acid belonging to the omega-9 fatty acid family. It is an 18-carbon fatty acid with one double bond, located at the ninth carbon from the methyl end of the carbon chain. The chemical formula for oleic acid is C18H34O2.Oleic acid is classified as a monounsaturated fatty acid because it has a single double bond in its carbon chain.

Alpha-ketoglutarate, also known as 2-oxoglutarate, is a key intermediate in the tricarboxylic acid (TCA) cycle, also known as the citric acid cycle or Krebs cycle. The TCA cycle is a central metabolic pathway that takes place in the mitochondria of eukaryotic cells and is responsible for the generation of energy in the form of ATP.Alpha-ketoglutarate is formed within the TCA cycle during the conversion of isocitrate to alpha-ketoglutarate. This reaction is catalyzed by the enzyme isocitrate dehydrogenase.

questions.explanation: Oleic acid is a monounsaturated fatty acid belonging to the omega-9 fatty acid family. It is an 18-carbon fatty acid with one double bond, located at the ninth carbon from the methyl end of the carbon chain. The chemical formula for oleic acid is C18H34O2.Oleic acid is classified as a monounsaturated fatty acid because it has a single double bond in its carbon chain. In chemical terms, oleic acid is classified as a monounsaturated omega-9 fatty acid, abbreviated with a lipid number of 18:1 cis-9, and a main product of Δ9-desaturase. It has the formula CH3(CH2)7CH=CH(CH2)7COOH

questions.text: The first amino acid taking part in protein synthesis is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Methionine, often abbreviated as Met, is the amino acid that universally starts protein synthesis. It is encoded by the start codon AUG in mRNA, which signals the beginning of translation in the ribosome. This makes Methionine the first amino acid incorporated into all newly synthesized proteins.

Arginine, abbreviated as Arg, is not the first amino acid in protein synthesis. It plays roles in the urea cycle and the synthesis of nitric oxide but is not involved in the initiation of protein synthesis.
Valine, abbreviated as Val, is an essential amino acid important for muscle growth and energy production but is not involved as the initial amino acid in protein synthesis.
Tryptophan, abbreviated as Tryp, is essential for the synthesis of serotonin and niacin but does not play a role as the starting amino acid in protein synthesis.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Methionine (Met), which is the amino acid that universally initiates protein synthesis in eukaryotic cells. It is recognized by the start codon AUG in mRNA, allowing the ribosome to initiate translation. The other options, Arginine (Arg), Valine (Val), and Tryptophan (Tryp), while important amino acids, do not serve this specific role in protein synthesis.

questions.text: Which one is an amino acid?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. Glycine is a simple amino acid that contains both an amino group (-NH2) and a carboxyl group (-COOH) attached to the same carbon atom, known as the alpha carbon.
This option is incorrect. Acetone is a ketone, not an amino acid. It lacks the amino and carboxyl groups necessary to classify it as an amino acid.
This option is incorrect. Glucose is a carbohydrate, not an amino acid. It does not contain an amino group and a carboxyl group attached to the same carbon atom.
This option is incorrect. Ethanol is an alcohol and lacks the characteristic amino and carboxyl groups that define an amino acid.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Glycine, as it is a basic amino acid with both an amino group (-NH2) and a carboxyl group (-COOH) attached to the alpha carbon. The other options are either carbohydrates, ketones, or alcohols, which do not have the defining structure of amino acids.

questions.text: The peptide linkage is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. CONH2 represents an amide functional group, not the peptide linkage between amino acids.
This option is incorrect. -COONH2 does not represent a peptide bond; it combines elements of carboxyl and amide groups incorrectly.

This option is correct. The peptide bond, also known as an amide bond, is characterized by the -CONH- linkage, formed between the carboxyl group of one amino acid and the amino group of another, releasing water in the process.
This option is incorrect. -CH-NH does not represent a peptide bond. It lacks the complete structure that includes the carbonyl (C=O) group.

questions.explanation: The peptide bond is a covalent linkage formed between the carboxyl group of one amino acid and the amino group of another amino acid. This reaction releases a molecule of water (H2O) and results in the formation of a -CONH- linkage, which is characteristic of peptide bonds. Option C is correct because it accurately depicts the structure of a peptide bond. Option A is incorrect because CONH2 is an amide group but not a peptide bond. Option B is incorrect as -COONH2 is not a valid structural representation of a peptide bond. Option D is incorrect because -CH-NH lacks the necessary carbonyl group present in peptides.

questions.text: The following one is smallest:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Maltose is a disaccharide sugar composed of two glucose molecules linked together. While smaller than polysaccharides like cellulose, it is larger than a single amino acid like glycine.

Glycine is an amino acid, which is one of the building blocks of proteins. It is the simplest and smallest amino acid, consisting of just two carbon atoms, making it smaller than both disaccharides and polysaccharides.
Cellulose is a polysaccharide, a complex carbohydrate made up of a long chain of glucose units. It is significantly larger than a single amino acid like glycine.
Cellobiose is a disaccharide composed of two glucose molecules. Like maltose, it is larger than a single amino acid such as glycine.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Glycine. Glycine is the smallest molecule among the options provided because it is a single amino acid, the basic building block of proteins, and is composed of just two carbon atoms. In contrast, maltose and cellobiose are disaccharides, each made up of two glucose molecules, which makes them larger in size. Cellulose is a polysaccharide made up of long chains of glucose units, making it significantly larger than glycine.

questions.text: At branching of molecules of starch, which glycosidic bond occurs?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The γ-1,4 glycosidic bond does not occur in starch. It is not relevant to the branching in starch molecules.
This option is incorrect. Although it features a 1,6 linkage, the correct bond for starch branching is α-1,6, not β-1,6.
This option is incorrect. The β-1,4 glycosidic bond is not involved in the branching of starch molecules. It is more characteristic of cellulose.
This option is correct. In starch, the α-1,6 glycosidic bond is responsible for the branching points in the amylopectin structure.

questions.explanation: In the structure of starch, branching occurs via α-1,6 glycosidic bonds. Amylopectin, a component of starch, has a branched structure due to these α-1,6 linkages. The other options are incorrect because they refer to different types of glycosidic bonds that are not involved in the branching of starch. For example, β-1,4 bonds are typical of cellulose, and γ-1,4 bonds are not relevant to carbohydrates.

questions.text: In amylose, glucose units are linked by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct because amylose is made up of glucose units connected by α-1,4 glycosidic bonds, which results in a mostly linear polymer. Unlike amylopectin, which has both α-1,4 and α-1,6 bonds and is highly branched, amylose’s linear structure allows it to form helical coils and makes it less soluble in water. This structural difference also affects how enzymes, like amylase, break it down during digestion.
This option is incorrect. Although starch contains both α-1,4 and α-1,6 linkages, the latter is not found in amylose but rather in amylopectin, which is the branched component of starch.
This option is incorrect. Amylose does not contain α-1,6 linkages, which are responsible for the branching found in amylopectin, not the linear structure of amylose.
This option is incorrect because amylose is characterized solely by α-1,4 linkages and does not contain α-1,6 linkages.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that in amylose, glucose units are linked by α-1,4 glycosidic bonds. This linear structure differentiates amylose from amylopectin, which contains both α-1,4 and α-1,6 linkages, the latter of which contributes to its branched structure. Options suggesting α-1,6 linkages or a combination of linkages are incorrect for amylose. The presence of only α-1,4 linkages is a defining feature of amylose.

questions.text: Which is an unbranched polymer?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cellulose is an unbranched polysaccharide consisting of a linear chain of β-D-glucose units linked by β-1,4-glycosidic bonds. This structure provides rigidity and strength to plant cell walls.
Glycogen is a highly branched polysaccharide that serves as a form of energy storage in animals and humans. Its branched structure allows for rapid release of glucose when needed.
Starch is composed of two glucose polymers: amylose and amylopectin. Amylose is mostly linear, but starch overall is not purely unbranched due to the presence of amylopectin, which is branched.
This option is incorrect because not all of the listed polymers are unbranched; only cellulose is.

questions.explanation: Cellulose is the correct answer because it is an unbranched polysaccharide consisting of a linear chain of glucose units linked by β-1,4-glycosidic bonds. This linear structure is crucial for its role in providing structural support to plant cells. In contrast, glycogen is highly branched, which makes it effective for energy storage in animals. Starch contains both amylose (which is mostly linear) and amylopectin (which is branched), so it is not entirely unbranched.

questions.text: The monomer units in starch are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pyranose fructose is incorrect because fructose, although it can form a pyranose ring, is not the monomer unit of starch. Starch is made up of glucose units, specifically in the α-D-glucose form.
While β-D-glucose is a form of glucose, it is not the monomer of starch. Starch is composed of α-D-glucose units.
Furanose refers to a sugar ring structure with a five-membered ring, which is not relevant as the monomer of starch. Starch is composed of glucose units in a pyranose form, specifically α-D-glucose.
α-D-glucose is correct. It is the monomer unit of starch, forming the polysaccharide through glycosidic linkages.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is α-D-glucose. Starch is a polysaccharide made up of glucose monomers. Specifically, it is composed of α-D-glucose units linked together. The α designation refers to the orientation of the hydroxyl group on the anomeric carbon which is important for the structure and function of starch. Other options like pyranose fructose, β-D-glucose, and furanose do not represent the monomer units of starch. Pyranose fructose and furanose are related to different sugar structures, while β-D-glucose is a different anomer of glucose not used in starch.

questions.text: Chitin forming exoskeleton in arthropods is second most abundant carbohydrate on this earth. It is a:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because chitin is not a storage polysaccharide and does not contain sulfur. Instead, it is a structural polysaccharide found in the exoskeletons of arthropods and the cell walls of fungi. Its backbone consists of N-acetylglucosamine units, which contain nitrogen, giving it rigidity and strength.
This option is correct. Chitin, found in the exoskeletons of arthropods, is a nitrogen-containing structural homopolysaccharide, making it the second most abundant carbohydrate on Earth.
This option is incorrect. Mucopolysaccharides, also known as glycosaminoglycans, are different from chitin and are typically found in connective tissues, not exoskeletons.
This option is incorrect. Chitin is a polysaccharide, not an oligosaccharide. It is a large, long-chain molecule designed for structural purposes.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that chitin is a nitrogen-containing structural homopolysaccharide. Chitin is a long-chain polymer of N-acetylglucosamine, a derivative of glucose, and it is used primarily for structural purposes in the exoskeletons of arthropods. The other options are incorrect because they do not accurately describe the composition or function of chitin: it is not a storage polysaccharide, it does not contain sulfur, it is not a mucopolysaccharide, and it is not an oligosaccharide.

questions.text: Which of the following carbohydrates gives a dark blue colour with iodine?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amylopectin is a branched polysaccharide and one of the two components of starch, with the other being amylose. While amylopectin is part of starch, it does not produce a dark blue color with iodine on its own. The characteristic color change is primarily due to amylose.

Starch is a polysaccharide that consists of amylose and amylopectin. The interaction of iodine with the helical structure of amylose in starch leads to the formation of a dark blue complex. This is the reason starch gives a dark blue color with iodine.

Cellulose is a polysaccharide composed of β-D-glucose units linked by β-1,4-glycosidic bonds. It does not react with iodine to form a dark blue color because its structure does not form helices, which are necessary for the iodine-starch complex.
This option is incorrect because one of the given substances, starch, does indeed react with iodine to produce a dark blue color.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Starch. Starch is composed of two types of glucose polymers: amylose and amylopectin. The iodine test for starch is based on the interaction between iodine and the helical structure of amylose, resulting in a dark blue color. Amylopectin, despite being part of starch, does not produce this color change on its own due to its highly branched structure. Cellulose does not form a complex with iodine because its linear chains do not create the necessary helical structure. Therefore, the statement 'None of these' is incorrect as starch does indeed produce a dark blue color with iodine.

questions.text: Which is incorrect regarding glycogen?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect, as glycogen is indeed analogous to starch. Both are storage polysaccharides, with glycogen being the form in animals and starch in plants.

This option is correct because glycogen is a storage polysaccharide, not a structural one. In animals, it is primarily stored in the liver and muscles, where it serves as a readily mobilizable energy reserve. Structurally, glycogen is highly branched, which allows rapid release of glucose when the body needs energy.
This option is incorrect, as glycogen is a non-reducing sugar, meaning it does not have a free aldehyde or ketone group capable of acting as a reducing agent.

options.text: It gives a red color with iodine solution
options.explanation: This option is incorrect, as glycogen gives a red color when treated with iodine solution, which is a characteristic test for the presence of glycogen.

questions.explanation: Glycogen is a storage polysaccharide found primarily in animals, stored in the liver and muscles. It is not a structural polysaccharide, which is a category that includes materials like cellulose in plants. Glycogen's role is similar to that of starch in plants, serving as an energy reserve. Glycogen is non-reducing, meaning it doesn't have a free aldehyde or ketone group to participate in redox reactions. Additionally, glycogen gives a red color when it reacts with iodine solution, which is a classic test for its presence.

questions.text: Immunoglobulins (antibodies) of the blood plasma are :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Immunoglobulins, or antibodies, are glycoproteins. They consist of protein chains with carbohydrate groups covalently attached, which are critical for their function and stability. Glycoproteins are involved in various biological processes, including immune response.
Flavoproteins contain flavin cofactors and play roles in redox reactions, not in immune responses. They are not related to the structural composition of immunoglobulins in blood plasma.
Lipoproteins are complexes of lipids and proteins and function primarily in lipid transport in the bloodstream. They do not play a direct role in the immune system as immunoglobulins do.
This option is incorrect because immunoglobulins are specifically glycoproteins. Flavoproteins and lipoproteins have distinct structures and functions unrelated to antibodies.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Glycoproteins, as immunoglobulins (antibodies) are glycoproteins. They have specific carbohydrate groups attached to their protein structure, which is essential for their function in the immune system. The other options are incorrect: Flavoproteins involve a flavin cofactor and are unrelated to immunoglobulins. Lipoproteins are involved in lipid transport and do not share the same structural characteristics as immunoglobulins. Therefore, the option 'All of these' is also incorrect, as it inaccurately groups different molecules with distinct functions and compositions.

questions.text: Most abundant protein on earth is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Keratin is a structural protein found in the skin, hair, and nails of animals. It is not involved in photosynthesis and is not the most abundant protein on Earth.
RuBP, or ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate, is a molecule involved in the Calvin cycle of photosynthesis. While important, it is not a protein and hence not the most abundant protein on Earth.

Rubisco, or ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase, is an enzyme that catalyzes the first major step of carbon fixation in the Calvin cycle of photosynthesis. It is the most abundant protein on Earth due to its crucial role in photosynthesis in plants, algae, and some bacteria.
Fibrinogen is a plasma protein involved in blood clotting. It is not involved in photosynthesis and is not the most abundant protein on Earth.

questions.explanation: Rubisco, or ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase, is the most abundant protein on Earth due to its essential role in the Calvin cycle of photosynthesis. It catalyzes the fixation of carbon dioxide, which is a critical process for the synthesis of organic compounds in plants, algae, and some bacteria. While keratin, RuBP, and fibrinogen are significant in their respective roles, they do not match Rubisco in abundance or function in photosynthesis.

questions.text: The protein of red muscles to store oxygen is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Haemoglobin is a protein found in red blood cells responsible for transporting oxygen from the lungs to various body tissues and carrying carbon dioxide back to the lungs for exhalation. It is not primarily involved in the storage of oxygen within muscles.
Myosin is a motor protein involved in muscle contraction and movement. It does not have a role in oxygen storage within muscles.

Myoglobin is a protein specifically found in muscle tissues, including red muscles, and is responsible for the storage and transport of oxygen within those tissues. It allows muscles to have a readily available supply of oxygen during heightened activity.
Actin is a protein that is part of the cytoskeleton and plays a role in cell structure and movement. It does not store or transport oxygen.

questions.explanation: Myoglobin is the correct answer because it is a protein found in muscle tissues that stores oxygen, providing a reserve that can be used during muscle contraction and periods of increased demand. While haemoglobin is involved in oxygen transport in the blood, it does not store oxygen within muscles. Myosin and actin are related to muscle contraction and cell structure, respectively, but do not have a role in oxygen storage.

questions.text: Formation of proteins is a type_.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Dehydration synthesis, also known as a condensation reaction, involves forming covalent bonds between molecules with the removal of a water molecule. This process is essential in forming proteins and other macromolecules.
Hydration synthesis is not a recognized term in biological chemistry. The process of adding water to break bonds is known as hydrolysis, not hydration synthesis.
Dehydrogenation involves the removal of hydrogen from molecules, often associated with oxidation reactions, and is unrelated to the formation of proteins.
Hydrogenation is the addition of hydrogen to molecules, typically used to saturate organic compounds, and does not pertain to protein synthesis.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Dehydration synthesis. This process is crucial in forming proteins, where amino acids are linked by peptide bonds through the removal of water molecules. The other options do not correctly describe this process: 'Hydration synthesis' is not a valid term, 'Dehydrogenation' involves removing hydrogen, and 'Hydrogenation' involves adding hydrogen, neither of which are related to protein formation.

questions.text: Natural silk fibre is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Polyester is a synthetic polymer made from petrochemicals, not a natural biological molecule. It is widely used in fabrics and textiles but is not derived from natural sources like silk.
Lipids are organic compounds such as fats and oils that are hydrophobic in nature. They are not the structural components of silk, which is derived from proteins.

Silk is primarily composed of proteins, specifically fibroin, which is produced by silkworms. Proteins are large, complex molecules made up of amino acids, and they form the structural basis of silk fibers.
Polysaccharides are carbohydrates made up of long chains of sugar molecules. While they serve as structural components in some organisms, silk fibers are not composed of polysaccharides.

questions.explanation: Silk is a natural fiber that is primarily composed of the protein fibroin, secreted by silkworms. This makes Option C, 'Protein,' the correct answer. Polyester (Option A) is a synthetic polymer, lipids (Option B) are fats and oils, and polysaccharides (Option D) are carbohydrates. None of these are the primary components of natural silk fibers.

questions.text: Keratin and chitin are chemically:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Keratin is a protein, while chitin is a carbohydrate, making them chemically different. They are, however, functionally similar since both provide structural support.
This option is incorrect. Although chitin is a carbohydrate, keratin is not; it is a protein. Both provide structural functions, making them functionally similar, not different.
This option is incorrect. Keratin is indeed a protein, but chitin is a carbohydrate. They serve similar structural roles despite their chemical differences.

This option is correct. Keratin and chitin are chemically different—keratin is a protein, and chitin is a carbohydrate. However, they are functionally similar as both play roles in providing structural support.

questions.explanation: Keratin and chitin are chemically different: keratin is a protein, typically found in hair, nails, and skin, while chitin is a carbohydrate, found in the exoskeletons of arthropods and the cell walls of fungi. Despite their chemical differences, they are functionally similar, as both are involved in providing structural support. The incorrect options either misidentify the chemical nature of keratin and chitin or incorrectly state their functional similarities.

questions.text: Structural proteins are usually:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Enzymatic proteins are involved in catalyzing biochemical reactions, not in providing structural support. They are typically globular in shape, allowing them to interact with substrates and catalyze reactions efficiently.

Fibrous proteins are known for their structural role in cells and tissues. They have an elongated, thread-like structure that makes them well-suited to provide support and strength. Examples include collagen and keratin.
Soluble proteins dissolve easily in water and are usually involved in dynamic processes such as transport and catalysis, rather than structural support. They often have a globular form.

Globular proteins are generally water-soluble and have diverse functions including catalysis, transport, and regulation. Their compact shape is not conducive to providing structural support like fibrous proteins.

questions.explanation: Fibrous proteins are the correct answer because they provide structural integrity to cells and tissues. They have a long, thread-like shape that is ideal for forming strong fibers and supporting frameworks. Unlike fibrous proteins, enzymatic proteins are primarily involved in facilitating chemical reactions, soluble proteins are involved in processes like transport and are water-soluble, and globular proteins have a more spherical shape and perform a variety of other biological functions.

questions.text: Glycosidic bond is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. A glycosidic bond is a covalent bond that connects two carbohydrate molecules through an oxygen atom, forming a C-O-C linkage. It is essential in creating complex carbohydrates like disaccharides and polysaccharides.
This option is incorrect. The >C=O structure represents a carbonyl group, which is present within monosaccharide units but does not link carbohydrates together.
This option is incorrect. The CONH structure is an amide bond found in proteins, linking amino acids, not carbohydrates.
This option is incorrect. The CHO structure is an aldehyde group found in some monosaccharides, but it does not form bonds between carbohydrate molecules.

questions.explanation: ﻿The correct answer is C-O-C, which signifies a glycosidic bond. This covalent bond is crucial for linking monosaccharide units via an oxygen bridge, forming larger carbohydrates such as disaccharides and polysaccharides. The other options represent different chemical groups: >C=O is a carbonyl group within monosaccharides, CONH is an amide bond found in proteins, and CHO is an aldehyde group present in some sugars, none of which are involved in the formation of glycosidic bonds.

questions.text: Which one of the following has no free aldehyde or ketone group?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fructose is a monosaccharide classified as a ketohexose because it contains a ketone group at the second carbon atom in its structure. This ketone group is free in the open-chain form, which allows it to participate in reactions typical of ketoses, such as forming glycosidic bonds or undergoing isomerization.

Sucrose is a disaccharide composed of glucose and fructose linked by a glycosidic bond. This bond covers the aldehyde group of glucose and the ketone group of fructose, leaving no free aldehyde or ketone groups.
Maltose is a disaccharide made of two glucose molecules connected by an α-1,4-glycosidic bond. One glucose unit has a free aldehyde group, classifying it as a reducing sugar.

Galactose is a monosaccharide classified as an aldohexose because it has an aldehyde group at the first carbon in its structure. This free aldehyde group allows it to participate in typical reactions of aldoses, such as forming glycosidic bonds or undergoing oxidation.

questions.explanation: Sucrose is the correct answer because it is a non-reducing sugar. In sucrose, the glycosidic bond between glucose and fructose involves the aldehyde group of glucose and the ketone group of fructose, meaning no free aldehyde or ketone groups are present. Fructose, maltose, and galactose all have free aldehyde or ketone groups. Fructose contains a free ketone group, maltose has a free aldehyde group from one glucose unit, and galactose contains a free aldehyde group, making all of them reducing sugars.

questions.text: Relationship between amino acid and protein is similar to one found between:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glucose and fructose are simple sugars that are isomers of each other, meaning they share the same molecular formula but differ in structure. They are not building blocks of a larger macromolecule, but rather individual sugar units.

Nucleosides consist of a nitrogenous base attached to a sugar molecule, but they lack the phosphate group required to form nucleotides. Therefore, they are not directly analogous to amino acids and proteins.

Nucleotides are the building blocks of nucleic acids, similar to how amino acids are the building blocks of proteins. Nucleotides consist of a nitrogenous base, a sugar, and a phosphate group, which link together to form the long chains of DNA or RNA.
Purines and pyrimidines are types of nitrogenous bases found in nucleotides. They are components of nucleotides, not direct analogs to amino acids and proteins.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: Nucleotides and nucleic acid. Nucleotides are the monomeric units that polymerize to form nucleic acids, just as amino acids polymerize to form proteins. Each nucleotide consists of a nitrogenous base, a sugar, and one or more phosphate groups, allowing them to link together to form the long chains characteristic of DNA and RNA. Other options, such as glucose and fructose, or purines and pyrimidines, do not represent a similar building block-to-polymer relationship.

questions.text: A peptide bond is formed between:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: An aldehyde group and an amino group
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because aldehyde groups are not involved in peptide bond formation. Peptide bonds specifically form between a carboxyl group of one amino acid and the amino group of another.

options.text: An aldehyde group and an ester group
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because peptide bonds do not involve aldehyde or ester groups. A peptide bond forms when the carboxyl group of one amino acid reacts with the amino group of another, resulting in a covalent bond that links amino acids together in proteins. This bond is specifically an amide linkage, not an aldehyde or ester.

options.text: An aldehyde group and a carboxyl group
options.explanation: This option is incorrect as peptide bonds are not formed between aldehyde and carboxyl groups. The correct functional groups involved are the carboxyl group of one amino acid and the amino group of another.
